agent.name: Sarah Mitchell
agent.notable_actions: Explained all three plan tiers clearly; surfaced Family plan unprompted when partner mentioned; flagged Samsung Frame TV issue in bug report; confirmed trial upgrade in-call

resolution.summary: All plan comparison questions answered. Trial upgraded to confirm Premium access. Family plan recommended for partner household. Samsung Frame TV lag reported and logged; workaround (Chromecast) provided. Customer satisfied — likely to convert to Family plan.

key_moments.description: Customer opens positively: 'It's pretty good actually'
key_moments.trigger: Trial experience has been satisfactory so far

key_moments.description: Customer raises Samsung Frame TV lag — first friction point
key_moments.trigger: App performance issue on TV despite phone working fine

key_moments.description: Customer acknowledges workaround with mild frustration: 'It's just annoying because the whole point of the TV app is convenience'
Customer closes positively: 'Appreciate it. OK, I think that's everything. Thanks Sarah.'
key_moments.trigger: All questions resolved; bug flagged for follow-up

pain_points.description: Samsung Frame TV (2021) experiences significant lag and mid-class video buffering. Phone app works fine. Engineering bug fix targeted for mid-March.
pain_points.verbatim_quote: The app on my Samsung TV is really laggy. Like, it takes ages to load and sometimes the video buffers mid-class.

motivations.description: Customer tried yoga and cycling classes — positive impression of production quality drove continued interest.
AI workout planner is a strong differentiator — customer explicitly wants structured programming, not random class browsing.
Comparing StreamFit ($19.99/$29.99) against FitFlow ($15) and Peloton (~$25). Price is a consideration but not the deciding factor.

competitor_mentions.name: FitFlow
competitor_mentions.detail: Currently comparing — FitFlow cited at $15/month as cheaper alternative. No strong preference either way yet.

competitor_mentions.name: Peloton
competitor_mentions.detail: Mentioned at ~$25/month as a comparison point. StreamFit Premium ($29.99) is slightly pricier.

intent.primary: Evaluate StreamFit plans before committing at end of free trial — comparing features and price across competitors
intent.secondary: Likely to convert to Family plan for partner household; one open technical issue (TV app) could affect decision

factors: Still in free trial — no commitment made yet
Samsung TV lag is a minor friction; workaround provided

signals: Partner also works out — Family plan saves money vs two separate subscriptions
Customer explicitly expressed intent: 'I'll probably go with Family'
AI workout planner is a genuine pull factor for Premium tier

action_items.action: Flag Samsung Frame TV (2021) in engineering bug report
Follow up with customer when Samsung TV app update goes live (mid-March target)
